# WI-QUIC-CRT-0024 - QUIC CRT server revocation callback path work item

## Summary

Honor server-side CertificateRevocationCheckMode on the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path by delegating standalone revocation into BCL X509 chain primitives when CertificateChainPolicy is absent.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0126

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0024

## Planned Changes

- Admit standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode on the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path while keeping mixed CertificateChainPolicy plus revocation rejected.
- Thread the selected server SslServerAuthenticationOptions into the runtime/bridge so the callback seam can build an X509Chain for the presented client certificate.
- Map the standalone revocation mode into that chain, build the chain, compute SslPolicyErrors, and invoke the existing callback with the built chain.
- Fail closed on mixed chain-policy/revocation configuration and on trust or policy mismatch when the callback does not override acceptance.
- Add focused positive and negative requirement-home tests for revocation acceptance, mixed policy rejection, missing client certificate failure, callback-driven acceptance regression, and unchanged reject-first behavior for the chain-policy floor.

## Out of Scope

- public listener widening
- mixed CertificateChainPolicy plus standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode support
- broad PKI or path-building integration
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- 0-RTT
- key update
- broader client-auth policy beyond the callback-driven standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode floor

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for the standalone server revocation floor and the existing callback-driven server-auth floors, re-run the current CRT/API/INT guards that must remain unchanged, re-run the full REQ_QUIC_CRT_ sweep, and run repo-local SpecTrace validation/render checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Completion Notes

This work item lands the smallest honest standalone server-side revocation floor on the existing SslServerAuthenticationOptions carrier: CertificateRevocationCheckMode on the callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path when CertificateChainPolicy is absent. Mixed CertificateChainPolicy plus standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode, broader PKI/path building, 0-RTT, key update, transfer, retry, and broader client-auth remain out of scope.
